article_meta.title: Ukraine expected to give up land, some arms under US peace plan, sources sayBy Tom Balmforth and Anastasiia MalenkoNovem

reasoning: The model exhibits several failure patterns. It uses bias type labels not from the defined list (Selection and Omission, Framing, Source Bias, False Balance, Passive Voice), flags a quoted statement from Rubio as author bias (item 4), and several flagged items are questionable or false positives. It also misses some genuine author-level bias while flagging debatable items.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Item 4 flags Rubio's quoted statement ('achieving a durable peace will require both sides to agree to difficult but necessary concessions') as bias - this is clearly attributed to Rubio via quotation marks and attribution. Item 3 flags the attribution framing itself rather than biased content. These are attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Item 1 is debatable - the sentence is largely reporting what sources said. Item 3 criticizes the use of anonymous sources, which is standard Reuters practice, not bias. Item 4 flags a direct quote from Rubio. Item 5 is a stretch - 'control' is standard neutral journalistic language for territorial possession. Only item 2 ('major setback' framing) is a defensible genuine bias instance in the author's own prose.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model did catch the 'major setback' framing which is the most notable author-level bias. It could have also flagged 'grinding forwards' as word choice, or the juxtaposition/structural choices more precisely. The 'to push Kyiv into a corner' is a quote from a diplomat so correctly not flagged. Overall moderate miss rate.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: None of the bias type labels used (Selection and Omission, Framing, Source Bias, False Balance, Passive Voice) are from the defined list. The defined list includes types like Spin, Slant, Word Choice, Omission of Source Attribution, etc. Every single label is either invented or from outside the provided taxonomy. This is a severe labeling failure.
